blogs.title: How to Choose the Right Facial for Your Skin
blogs.excerpt: A clear guide to brightening, hydrating, calming, and event-ready facial choices.
blogs.content: The best facial is not the most intense one. It is the one that matches your current skin state, event timeline, and home routine. For dullness, choose gentle exfoliation and vitamin support. For dryness, prioritize barrier repair and massage. For sensitivity, keep the session calm and avoid stacking actives. If you are preparing for an event, schedule a consultation first and keep your final facial several days before makeup.
blogs.author: Dr. Mira Sen

seo.title: How to Choose the Right Facial | Skincare Guide
seo.description: Learn how to select the right facial for dullness, dryness, sensitivity, and events.

blogs.title: How Often Should You Book a Massage for Stress Relief?
blogs.excerpt: A practical schedule for occasional tension, high-stress seasons, and maintenance.
blogs.content: For general maintenance, one massage every three to four weeks can help keep tension from becoming your default posture. During high-stress periods, a short weekly or fortnightly session can be more useful than one very intense session. Pair massage with hydration, gentle movement, and sleep boundaries so the benefits have somewhere to land.
blogs.author: Aditi Nair

seo.title: Massage Frequency for Stress Relief
seo.description: A practical massage schedule for stress relief, tension, and monthly wellness maintenance.

blogs.title: A Calm Bridal Beauty Timeline
blogs.excerpt: A 12-week plan for facials, hair spa, body polish, and event-week care.
blogs.content: Start with consultation and gentle consistency. Twelve weeks out, map skin concerns and hair goals. Eight weeks out, begin facial and hair spa rhythm. Four weeks out, avoid new aggressive treatments. Event week is for polish, hydration, and rest. The goal is not transformation overnight; it is steady, calm preparation.
blogs.author: Leena Bose

seo.title: Bridal Beauty Timeline | Spa and Salon Prep
seo.description: A calm 12-week bridal timeline for skin, hair, body polish, and wellness prep.

blogs.title: Building an At-Home Spa Ritual That You Will Actually Keep
blogs.excerpt: Simple sensory cues and product pairings for a weekly reset.
blogs.content: A ritual becomes repeatable when it is short, sensory, and easy to begin. Keep a room mist, soak salts, towel, and one body product together. Dim the lights, take a warm shower or bath, moisturize slowly, and stop before it becomes a project. The point is not perfection; it is a reliable pause.
blogs.author: Samir Arora

seo.title: At-Home Spa Ritual | Wellness Tips
seo.description: Build a simple at-home spa ritual with bath salts, room mist, and body care.
